published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation gives statistic-specific sharp sensitivity tests; HuberStrassen1973Minimax supplies the general representative-pair template.

HuberStrassen1973Minimax gives finite robust-testing least-favorable-distribution machinery; CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame studies a closely related matched-study adversarial testing game over a restricted test class.

structure_flags.label: proposal kernel / target_estimand
structure_flags.one_line: \(\beta_n^\star\) is power for a test of assignment vectors under an artificial assignment alternative, not an estimation or inference result for a causal estimand required by the Stat cluster.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The closer Cohen, Olson, and Fogarty (2020), “Multivariate one-sided testing in matched observational studies as an adversarial game,” Biometrika 107(4):809–825, is omitted; it must be distinguished from an all-tests finite saddle point.

novelty_flags.label: Fogarty2020Adversarial
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited bibliographic item is wrong: the adversarial-game paper is Cohen, Olson, and Fogarty (2020), Biometrika 107(4):809–825, not a sole-author Fogarty paper titled “Multivariate sensitivity analysis” in 107(3):633–648.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 and Open-ended Question 1
soundness_flags.one_line: \(Q\) defines a new assignment law and \(\phi\) observes only \(Z\); neither is linked to potential-outcome alternatives or an outcome statistic, so the sharp-null and causal-sensitivity claims do not support the stated power frontier or its applied consumers.

soundness_flags.label: ass:sharp-null
soundness_flags.one_line: The frontier, null polytope, alternative law, and all claimed equalities are functions only of \((Z,Q,\Gamma)\), so the Fisher sharp null is mathematically unused unless the proposal specifies how outcomes generate the testing problem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rebuild the target as a causal randomization/sensitivity test: specify the observed outcome statistic and a potential-outcome alternative that induces its distribution, then derive \(Q\) from those primitives rather than postulating an alternative assignment law.

Correct and expand related work with CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame and explicitly state why its coherent-linear-class adversarial optimization neither subsumes nor yields the proposed all-tests finite least-favorable mixture.

Replace the broad mixture request with a worked \(n=1,m=3\) theorem giving explicit mixture weights and a non-tautological support-switch inequality in \((Q,\Gamma,\alpha)\), then generalize that object.

recommended_next_step: Revise the causal data-generating/testing link and literature position before advancing; retain the finite robust-testing kernel only after it is tied to an outcome-based causal sensitivity problem.
